title: oreosedum album subsp. micranthum
intro: Oreosedum album subsp. micranthum, also known as small-flowered oreosedum, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, Oreosedum album subsp. micranthum is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
